obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG pins, debug mode input, debug request/response handshakes, dmi_jtag/SimJTAG instances, and jtag_hash/jtag_unlock signals. Debug access is security-sensitive and must not be treated as safe merely because debug logic exists; verify that all reachable debug operations are covered by authorization.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/DTM or simulation debug interface
obligations.operation: trigger debug entry/reset or read/write debug-module resources
obligations.object: hart debug control, ndmreset_o/dmactive_o/debug_req_o, and debug module bus-visible state
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization/unlock (e.g. jtag_unlock or equivalent authentication) must mediate debug requests before they can request hart debug entry, non-debug-module reset, or access protected system state
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM inputs -> dmi_jtag/SimJTAG/Debug Module signals -> debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o and any dm_slave/dm_master access path
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default of jtag_unlock or debug authorization state
whether debug_mode_i bypasses or substitutes for authorization
whether SimDTM/SimJTAG test paths are synthesis-gated or otherwise mediated
required_evidence_slots: operation: DMI/debug request op or reset/debug entry
object: debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o or bus slave/master state
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, path/reachability
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists signals and instances but not complete debug-module connections; inspect implementation around the cited signals.

obligations.reason: The top-level contains acc_ctrl, acc_ctrl_c indexed by privilege level/peripheral, and many protected-looking peripheral NoC endpoints. The assignment acc_ctrl_c[i][j] includes a special OR condition for one peripheral index, so each visible peripheral request should be checked for correct access mediation.
obligations.subject: CPU/NoC MMIO requester at a given priv_lvl_i
obligations.operation: route/read/write MMIO or NoC request to peripheral
obligations.object: peripheral request paths and protected peripheral address regions
obligations.expected_guard: peripheral accesses must be gated by the intended privilege/access-control bit for the requester and target peripheral; aliasing between peripheral indices must be intentional and safe
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals access-control decode -> acc_ctrl/acc_ctrl_c -> peripheral request enables, especially rom_req, crypto/RNG/debug/DMA/reglk/reset peripheral NoC paths
state_conditions_to_check: reset/update source of acc_ctrl bits
whether acc_ctrl can be programmed by insufficiently privileged software
special case acc_ctrl_c[i][j] OR behavior for j==5 using index 4
whether read and write paths share the same guard or need distinct guards
knowledge_used: Common false-positive risks: explain expected guard
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer is truncated for later assignments; confirm all request enable equations in the full file.

obligations.reason: The design exposes a wide reglk_ctrl vector and dma_wrapper has reglk_ctrl_i plus core_lock_reg and write-enable logic. Verify lock enforcement at the actual write sites rather than assuming the lock exists.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester or alternate debug/test writer
obligations.operation: write or reconfigure locked peripheral registers
obligations.object: DMA configuration registers and other peripheral configuration controlled by reglk_ctrl
obligations.expected_guard: once a lock bit is set for a protected peripheral/register, all software/debug/test write paths that modify the protected configuration must be blocked until permitted reset or lifecycle event
obligations.candidate_path: reglk_ctrl register source -> peripheral wrapper lock inputs -> write enables for DMA and other protected configuration registers
state_conditions_to_check: reset value of lock bits
set and clear conditions for locks
whether locks are sticky or can be cleared by software
whether we_flag/testmode/rst_N signals bypass lock
whether read-only status registers are incorrectly protected/unprotected
required_evidence_slots: object: start/length/source/dest/core_lock or target peripheral register
obligations.uncertainty: Need full procedural blocks in dma_wrapper and top-level register-lock peripheral to map lock bits to registers.

obligations.reason: The DMA has programmable start/length/source/destination registers and PMP-related state (pmp_addr_reg, pmp_access_type_en, pmp_allow_reg, abort, valid_reg). A DMA engine can bypass CPU permission checks if its transfer generation is not mediated by PMP or equivalent range checks.
obligations.subject: DMA engine / software configuring DMA
obligations.object: memory regions reachable by DMA source/destination addresses
obligations.expected_guard: DMA transfer start and each source/destination address used for memory access must be authorized by PMP/range/access-control checks before asserting transfer-valid state
obligations.candidate_path: DMA MMIO registers/start -> dma internal start/length/source/dest -> valid_o/transfer request generation -> PMP allow/abort logic
state_conditions_to_check: whether both source and destination are checked
whether checks cover entire length, not only base address
whether active transfer can continue after PMP/access configuration changes
whether pmp_allow_reg is reset safely and refreshed per transfer/address
whether abort reliably suppresses valid_o and clears outstanding state
required_evidence_slots: subject: DMA engine configured through MMIO
object: memory address range from source/dest/length
expected guard: PMP/range allow for relevant access type
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer gives internal signals but not state-machine details; inspect transfer FSM and PMP instantiations.

obligations.reason: dma_wrapper exposes AXI input/output ports, reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, we_flag, rst_8, and a set of DMA registers. The visible RTL should show that writes are not accepted solely from address decode/write strobe without permission mediation.
obligations.subject: AXI-lite requester targeting DMA wrapper
obligations.object: DMA software-visible configuration and control registers
obligations.expected_guard: DMA configuration writes must require the intended MMIO access permission/accounting control and register-lock state before updating transfer parameters or start control
obligations.candidate_path: dma_wrapper AXI-lite decode -> start_reg/length_reg/source_addr_*_reg/dest_addr_*_reg/core_lock_reg writes -> dma instance inputs
state_conditions_to_check: reset values of DMA config registers
whether start can be asserted without permitted configuration writes
whether partial writes/byte enables bypass guards
whether we_flag or rst_8 creates a privileged override
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: acct_ctrl_i plus reglk_ctrl_i/core_lock and valid decode
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect of address decode and procedural updates around line 129 onward.

obligations.reason: The AXI-lite interface produces address_o, en_o, we_o, and data_o from AXI handshakes. Permission analysis should check whether downstream register writes are based on current valid requests and not on stale address_n/trans_id state or mismatched read/write channel handshakes.
obligations.subject: AXI master connected to axi_lite_interface
obligations.operation: read/write downstream register via AXI-lite bridge
obligations.object: downstream CLINT/wrapper registers selected by address_o/en_o/we_o
obligations.expected_guard: AXI-lite conversion must preserve valid address/write intent and any upstream permission attributes; protected downstream registers must not be written merely due to stale address or incomplete handshake
obligations.candidate_path: axi_lite_interface AXI channels -> en_o/we_o/address_o/data_o -> CLINT or wrapper register access
state_conditions_to_check: reset of address_n/trans_id_n
stability of address during en_o/we_o
handling of simultaneous or reordered read/write requests
whether assertions only check protocol and not permission
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: valid AXI handshake plus downstream permission guard
obligations.uncertainty: The bridge is generic; finding requires tying it to a protected downstream register in this scope.

obligations.reason: The PMP modules receive addr_i and riscv PMP configuration-like inputs and emit allow_o/match_o. Since DMA depends on PMP-like allow state, permission correctness requires checking default deny, priority, matching and access-type mediation.
obligations.subject: CPU/DMA memory requester
obligations.expected_guard: PMP allow must be false unless an enabled entry matches the requested address and grants the requested access type for the current privilege/requester; address matching must implement correct bounds for TOR/NA4/NAPOT or supported modes
obligations.candidate_path: PMP configuration inputs -> pmp_entry match_o -> pmp allow_o -> DMA/CPU access decision
state_conditions_to_check: no-entry-match default behavior
entry priority when multiple match bits asserted
lock bit effects on PMP entries if present
address granularity and boundary conditions
privilege-mode exceptions or machine-mode behavior
required_evidence_slots: subject: memory requester using PMP decision
operation: read/write/execute or DMA data access
object: address range under PMP control
expected guard: matching PMP entry with access permission and privilege conditions
observed allow_o equation and match priority
obligations.uncertainty: Fact extraction labels several PMP ports generically as riscv; inspect declarations to identify config, access type and privilege inputs.

obligations.reason: The top-level contains fuse_req, fuse_addr, and fuse_rdata signals. Fuses are protected assets when they contain keys, identities, lifecycle or security configuration; verify an actual exposure path and guard before reporting.
obligations.subject: software-visible requester or debug/test interface
obligations.expected_guard: fuse read/exposure must be limited to authorized subjects and fuse-derived security configuration must not be readable or overridden by unprivileged/debug/test paths
obligations.candidate_path: fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata path -> NoC/MMIO response or security configuration consumers
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default value of fuse response registers
whether stale fuse_rdata remains readable after denied request
whether debug/test path can read fuse_rdata
whether acc_ctrl/reglk controls cover fuse address space
required_evidence_slots: subject: MMIO/NoC/debug requester or internal consumer
operation: read fuse or override fuse-backed policy
external response path or security-control effect
obligations.uncertainty: Only signal names are visible; inspect around line 1613 and response muxes for reachability.

obligations.reason: The top-level includes many per-peripheral reset signals and a reset NoC endpoint; dma_wrapper receives rst_8. Permission analysis should check reset write paths and reset effects on locks/configuration.
obligations.subject: software/NoC/debug requester controlling reset
obligations.operation: trigger peripheral reset or clear security state
obligations.object: peripheral reset lines and security/lock/configuration state affected by reset
obligations.expected_guard: writes that assert peripheral resets must be privilege/access-control guarded, and reset must not clear locks or security state in a way that permits unauthorized reconfiguration
obligations.candidate_path: reset-control peripheral/NoC path -> rst_1..rst_13 and rst_8 into dma_wrapper -> protected peripheral reset behavior
state_conditions_to_check: whether reset lines are writable by unprivileged subjects
whether reset clears reglk/core_lock while preserving access to target config
whether security-sensitive registers retain stale values or become unsafe defaults
whether debug/ndmreset interacts with peripheral resets
required_evidence_slots: subject: MMIO/NoC requester writing reset control
object: rst_N signal and affected protected registers/locks
expected guard: privilege/access-control and lock/lifecycle policy
observed reset generation and register reset behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Need decode and update logic for reset peripheral in full top-level file.

obligations.reason: The top-level and DMA modules expose testmode_i and multiple we_flag inputs. Such signals may be legitimate but must be checked for bypass of permission guards and lock enforcement.
obligations.subject: test/manufacturing override interface
obligations.operation: override write protection or force write/reset/control state
obligations.object: protected configuration registers, lock state, DMA/PMP control state
obligations.expected_guard: test or manufacturing override signals must not enable writes, unlocks, resets, or protected operations in normal operation unless gated by explicit test lifecycle/debug authorization
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i/we_flag_0..we_flag_4/we_flag signals -> peripheral wrappers and DMA internal write enables
state_conditions_to_check: whether testmode_i is tied off or externally controllable in this scope
whether we_flag bypasses reglk_ctrl/acct_ctrl/PMP checks
reset defaults of override-controlled registers
whether override effects persist after test mode is deasserted
required_evidence_slots: object: protected register or permission state affected
expected guard: test lifecycle enable or debug authorization plus normal lock/permission guard
observed use of testmode_i/we_flag in assignments/procedural blocks
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists we_flag ports but not all uses; inspect all occurrences.
